item.title: European powers urge Israel to end settlements
item.description: Italy, France, Britain and Germany urged Israel Friday to stop expanding its settlements in the West Bank, saying the government was ‘undermining stability’ and prospects for a two-state solution....

item.title: 26 AL activists held in city for trying to take ‘oath’ for Hasina return
item.description: The police on Friday detained 26 activists of the Bangladesh Awami League, activities on which remain banned, at places of Dhanmondi in Dhaka on Friday on charge of trying to hold an oath-taking event demanding the return of Sheikh Hasina....

item.title: Tension erupts along border
item.description: Tensions along Bangladesh borders with India erupted afresh due to a sudden rise in border killings by the Indian Border Security Force, pushing people into Bangladesh from India, the newly elected West Bengal government’s allocation of land for......

item.title: Highway gridlocks feared during Eid rush
item.description: The rush of people leaving the capital, Dhaka, for different areas across the country to celebrate Eid-ul-Azha is expected to start in a couple of days amid hassles such as traffic congestion caused by road construction works, cattle markets on or......

item.title: Laundering thru crypto leaves Bangladesh chasing shadows
item.description: In a country like Bangladesh, from where assets travel abroad in many crooked ways and hardly come back, cryptocurrency is another headache as money is being laundered through this dimly-lit alleyway, investigators have found. ...

item.title: India’s strategic $9b megaport plan for pristine island
item.description: On a remote island in the Andaman Sea, bulldozers are tearing into pristine forests that are home to one of Earth’s most isolated people — part of India’s ambition for a $9 billion megaport, airport and city....

item.title: Canada oil poised for key role in US trade talks
item.description: At the heavy equipment repair business Corey Coffey runs in Canada’s oil hub, the future felt precarious when President Donald Trump returned to office last year....

item.title: Bank Asia signs deal with BB to finance CMSMEs
item.description: Bank Asia PLC on Monday signed an agreement with the Bangladesh Bank to facilitate and strengthen financing support for cottage, micro, small and medium enterprises across the country, said a press release....

item.title: NCC Bank hosts free eye care camp in Feni
item.description: On the occasion of its 33rd founding anniversary, NCC Bank has undertaken various social responsibility initiatives, said a press release....

item.title: Midland Bank signs refinance agreement with BB
item.description: Midland Bank PLC on Monday signed a refinance participation agreement with Bangladesh Bank to implement the Tk 3,000-crore Cluster Financing Scheme and the Tk 1,500-Crore Financial Sector Fund for the Development of Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (FSFDMSME)....

item.title: Prime Bank okays 30pc dividends
item.description: Shareholders of Prime Bank PLC on Thursday approved 25 per cent cash dividend and 5 per cent stock dividend for the year ended December 31, 2025, along with the audited financial statements....

item.title: Community Bank’s entrepreneurship dev prog concludes
item.description: Community Bank Bangladesh PLC, in collaboration with the Bangladesh Bank, has concluded a ‘Entrepreneurship Development Programme’ held under the ‘Skills for Industry Competitiveness and Innovation Programme,’ said a press release...

item.title: MBTI hosts money AML, CFT training
item.description: Mercantile Bank Training Institute has recently organised a comprehensive training programme on ‘Prevention of Money Laundering and Combating Financing of Terrorism’ in Rajshahi, said a press release....

item.title: Banks open today
item.description: All scheduled banks across the country will remain open on Saturday and Sunday, maintaining regular banking operations and transaction hours as usual ahead of the upcoming Eid-ul-Azha holidays....

item.title: The urgent need for a united Arab initiative
item.description: US PRESIDENT Donald Trump’s state visit to China will go down in history as the day the United States finally acknowledged Beijing’s ascendancy as a global superpower. That acknowledgment does not need to be articulated in a formal statement; it......

item.title: Development and dispossession
item.description: AS ROADS expand, flyovers rise, rail corridors are cleared and urban beautification accelerates, slum communities in Dhaka are often the first to bear the burden of ‘progress.’ Under the banner of development and modernisation, informal dwellers are......City & Local Guides

item.title: Open letter to members of parliament
item.description: TIME moves like dust. It lifts, drifts. It settles. Everything — memories of glee and sorrow, memories that had lodged themselves in the chest and the skull, layer upon patient layer — enveloped under a blanket of dust. The proud moments of a......

item.title: When even children aren’t safe
item.description: HER shoes were outside the neighbour’s door. Her mother knocked. Nobody answered. When other neighbours forced it open, they found the eight-year-old girl’s torso at a place and the head at another place. She had been a topper in Popular......

item.title: Justice dies before the victim is buried
item.description: The rape and murder of an eight-year-old girl at Mirpur in Dhaka are not merely another headline in Bangladesh’s worsening law-and-order crisis; they are a mirror held up to a wounded state. The horrible incident stands as a symbol of fragile safety......

item.title: Sustainable restoration of Dhaka canals must
item.description: THE terrible state of canals in Dhaka exposes the chronic failure of the authorities to protect the ecological lifelines despite repeated promises and costly restoration projects. The canals that once sustained local ecosystems, carried rainwater and......

item.title: Govt needs to fix justice system to end rape, murder
item.description: A SURGE in violence against children, including rape and murder, questions the state’s safeguards for children’s safety. The rape and murder of a minor girl in Dhaka that took place on May 19 has again brought to the fore weaknesses in the child......Bangladesh Travel Guide

item.title: China probes ‘illegal’ brokers in cross-border trade crackdown
item.description: China’s market regulator announced a sweeping investigation on Friday against three major brokers running cross-border trading, as it launched a two-year crackdown on investment leaving the country....

item.title: California governor orders a plan to cope with AI job upheaval
item.description: California governor Gavin Newsom on Thursday ordered officials to start work on a plan to mitigate the job-destroying impact of artificial intelligence, the first US state to do so....

item.title: Giant wind turbine rises in Germany amid far-right headwinds
item.description: A wind turbine billed as the world’s tallest is rising in eastern Germany, winning praise as a beacon for a clean, green energy future and headwinds from the far-right AfD party....

item.title: Global stocks gain, oil wavers
item.description: Stocks pushed higher Friday while oil prices were little changed as traders tracked headlines on the chances for peace in the Middle East, against a backdrop of corporate earnings optimism and AI enthusiasm that have pushed indexes to record highs....

item.title: Woman held on charge of killing baby daughter in Sylhet
item.description: A woman allegedly killed her one-and-a-half-year-old daughter ​​​​by slitting her throat in Sylhet on Friday early morning....

item.title: Elderly man held over allegations of raping daughter-in-law
item.description: A 65-year-old Mizanur Rahman was arrested allegedly for his involvement in raping his daughter-in-law....

item.title: 12 injured in clash over control of cattle market in Munshiganj
item.description: At least 12 people were injured in a clash on Friday between two rival groups over the lease of a cattle market, traders’ association elections and local dominance at Sirajdikhan upazila of Munshiganj....

item.title: Jamaat opposes home minister’s comments on punishment for rapists
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami on Friday condemned the comment of home minister Salahuddin Ahmed for his remarks that calling for ‘death penalty openly’ for rapists as the highest punishment is a form of ‘medieval brutality’....

item.title: Two children of a family drown in Sylhet canal
item.description: Two minor children of a family drowned in a canal at Jaintapur upazila in Sylhet on Friday afternoon....

item.title: 16 shipping ministry officials face stand release over absenteeism, late attendance
item.description: The shipping ministry has issued ‘stand release’ orders against 16 officials and employees over allegations of absence from the workplace and late attendance....

item.title: SSC examinee attempts suicide over video circulation threat
item.description: A Secondary School Certificate examinee on Thursday made a suicide attempt over fear of circulating video showing her in unclothed state captured forcibly by a gang of miscreants in Narail....

item.title: Deteriorating law and order tarnishes govt image: discussion
item.description: Politicians, academics, and professionals at a discussion on Friday said that the deteriorating law and order situation, particularly the rise in the assault on women and children across the country, was tarnishing the image of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government....

item.title: DB arrests two over transfer, promotion scam
item.description: Detectives have arrested two alleged members of an organised fraud ring accused of using the names of key ministries, including the Home Ministry, to extort money by promising government officials transfers, postings and promotions....

item.title: 7 ‘honey trap’ gang members arrested in Barishal, sent to jail
item.description: A Barishal metropolitan magistrate court on Friday sent seven members of a ‘honey trap’ gang, including three women, to jail after the police arrested them in the Sadhur Battola area under the Kaunia police station in Barishal city on the night of May 21....

item.title: JCD expels two BU unit leaders over extortion allegations
item.description: Two leaders of the Barishal University unit of Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, ruling party BNP’s student wing, have been expelled from their organisational posts over allegations of involvement in extortion from fish transport vehicles....

item.title: Smriti 3355 maize seeds cause losses to farmers
item.description: More than 150 farmers in Lalmonirhat have allegedly suffered severe losses after cultivating the Smriti 3355 maize variety, with many claiming that the crop failed despite promises of high yields....

item.title: Erdogan shuts liberal Istanbul university
item.description: Turkish president Recep Tayyip Erdogan on Friday ordered the closure of a top independent university with liberal leanings in the middle of the school year, according to an official decree....

item.title: Desh TV reporter, cameraperson attacked by ‘drug gang’ at Savar
item.description: A television reporter and his cameraperson were seriously injured in an alleged attack by suspected drug dealers while covering a story at Savar on the outskirts of Dhaka on Friday, the police and local people said....

item.title: India seeks to suppress ‘Cockroach’ youth party
item.description: Indian authorities have sought to block the social media handles of the satirical ‘Cockroach People’s Party’, formed last week in response to comments from the country’s top judge seen as critical of young people....

item.title: SpaceX postpones highly anticipated Starship launch
item.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX postponed the highly anticipated launch of its upgraded Starship megarocket, calling off Thursday’s test after multiple countdown stops-and-starts....

item.title: Dhaka-Ctg Highway sees heavy traffic
item.description: Traffic pressure has intensified on the 105-kilometre Cumilla stretch of the Dhaka-Chattogram Highway as many people started heading home to celebrate Eid-ul-Azha with their near and dear ones, triggering slow movement, long tailbacks and prolonged travel times....

item.title: BGB clarifies report on ‘push-in’ of 9 Bangladeshi thru Sylhet border
item.description: The Border Guard Bangladesh on Friday issued a clarification regarding a report published in New Age on May 21 about the alleged ‘push-in’ of nine Bangladeshi nationals from India into Bangladesh....

item.title: CUET signs memo with Jasim Uddin Foundation
item.description: Jasim Uddin Foundation will provide four buses to Chittagong University of Engineering and Technology....

item.description: The Institutional Quality Assurance Cell of Chittagong University held a seminar titled Intellectual Property Rights: Linking Sports, Arts and Science, celebrating World Intellectual Property Day 2026, at the auditorium of the faculty of business......

item.title: New DUET VC felicitated
item.description: The newly appointed vice-chancellor of Dhaka University of Engineering and Technology Professor Mohammad Iqbal was felicitated at a ceremony at DUET in Gazipur on Thursday....

item.title: ULAB concludes BBA prog’s summer 2026 induction week
item.description: The bachelor of business administration programme of the University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh concluded its summer 2026 induction week at the ULAB in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: Biman completes pre-Hajj flights
item.description: The Biman Bangladesh Airlines completed its pre-Hajj flight operations 2026 on Thursday....

item.description: The Santras Nirmul Taqi Mancha organised a protest programme at the South Plaza of Sangsad Bhaban on Manik Mia Avenue in the capital, as the trial remains incomplete even 13 years after the murder of Tanvir Muhammad Taqi, said a press release....

item.title: Film songs promote Dhaka film industry: discussion
item.description: Film activists and educators, including veteran composer Sheikh Sadi Khan, vocalist Rafiqul Alam, Film movement activist and researcher Md Fokhrul Alam Shohag and actor-producer Helal Khan, stated that while film music historically promoted the......

item.title: British Council publishes new research on Bangladesh
item.description: The British Council and the Bengal Institute for Architecture, Landscapes and Settlements launched a research and insight publication titled Sustainable Architecture Practice at the Bengal Shilpalay of Dhanmondi in Dhaka on Friday....

item.title: ‘Songs of Fired Earth’ promotes Bangladesh’s folk pottery heritage
item.description: A five-day exhibition of rare and traditional clay artefacts, collected by writer, researcher, and journalist Imran Uz-Zaman, concluded on Friday at La Galerie of Alliance Française de Dhaka....

item.title: Shilpakala Academy celebrates Nazrul’s birth anniversary
item.description: Bangladesh Shilpakala Academy launched a three-day event on Friday at its National Theatre Hall to celebrate the 127th birth anniversary of national poet Kazi Nazrul Islam....

item.title: 15 injured as BNP factions in Gazipur clash over extortion
item.description: At least 15 people were injured in a clash between two rival factions of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party on Friday over control of a garment factory’s jhut (fabric waste) business and alleged extortion of worker transport buses in Gazipur....

item.title: BAD provides treatment to CHT people at Kaptai
item.description: The Bangladesh Academy of Dermatology conducted a free health campaign among the underprivileged people at the Chandraghona Christian Missionary Hospital under Kaptai in Rangamati on Thursday....

item.title: ICT minister for ensuring access to digital services for disabled people
item.description: Posts, telecommunications and information technology minister Faqir Mahbub Anam has said that ensuring equal access to digital services for persons with disabilities is one of the government’s key priorities....

item.title: Case filed over rape attempt on minor girl in Ctg
item.description: A case was filed early Friday over an alleged attempt to rape a three-and-a-half-year-old girl at Bakalia in Chattogram city....
